title: Wet-transition crossover specialist
summary: Wet races behave differently from slick stints: the key decision is the crossover point, and being one lap early or late can cost several seconds.
rationale: Wet races behave differently from slick stints: the key decision is the crossover point, and being one lap early or late can cost several seconds. Official F1/Pirelli material highlights that intermediate/full-wet/slick transitions are governed by crossover timing, not normal stint aging.
implementation_hint: Create a separate model or gating branch for rows on INTERMEDIATE/WET or races showing mixed compound states. Features: fraction of field on wet-weather tyres that lap, fraction already back to slicks, same-lap compound diversity, peer lap-time improvement rate, and race-progress interaction. Also test a binary 'transition regime' feature for dry compounds immediately after wet laps.
expected_effect: Should recover edge cases where current dry-tyre heuristics mis-rank rain crossover laps and mixed-condition stops.
risk: Sample size for true wet transitions may be small, so the specialist should be shallow and only activated when compound/race context clearly indicates a crossover regime.
